title: Charmed Life — 4 (Fragment 6)
sourceText:
Then, as Cat had feared, Mr. Saunders flew into a towering rage. His face went red. He slammed his big, knobby hand down on Cat’s desk so that Cat jumped and the inkwell jumped too, sending ink splashing over Mr. Saunders’ great hand and over Cat’s essay. “Left-handed!” he roared. “Then why the Black Gentleman don’t you write with your left hand, boy?”

“They—they punish me if I do,” Cat faltered, very shaken, and very perplexed to find Mr. Saunders was angry for such a peculiar reason.

“Then they deserve to be tied up in knots and roasted!” roared Mr. Saunders, “whoever they are! You’re doing yourself untold harm by obeying them, boy! If I catch you writing with your right hand again, you’ll be in really serious trouble!”

“Yes,” Cat said, relieved but still very shaken. He looked mournfully at his ink-splashed essay and hoped Mr. Saunders might use a little witchcraft on that too. But Mr. Saunders took the book and tore the page right out.

“Now do it again properly!” he said, slapping the book back in front of Cat.

Cat was still writing all over again about Canute when Mary came in with a tray of milk and biscuits and a cup of coffee for Mr. Saunders. And after the milk and biscuits, Mr. Saunders told Cat and Gwendolen they were free till lunch. “Though not because of a good morning’s work,” he said. “Go out and get some fresh air.” As they went out of the schoolroom, he turned to Roger and Julia. “Now we’ll have a little witchcraft,” he said. “And let’s hope you haven’t forgotten all that too.”

Gwendolen stopped in the doorway and looked at him.

“No. Not you,” Mr. Saunders said to her. “I told you.”

Gwendolen whirled around and ran away, through the shabby playroom and down the corridor beyond. Cat ran after her as hard as he could, but he did not catch up with her until they came to a much grander part of the Castle, where a big marble staircase curled away downward and the light came from an elegant dome in the roof.

“This isn’t the right way,” Cat panted.

“Yes it is,” Gwendolen said fiercely. “I’m going to find Chrestomanci. Why should those two fat little fools learn witchcraft and not me? I’ve got twice their gifts. It took two of them just to levitate a jug of cocoa! So I want Chrestomanci.”

By a stroke of good fortune, Chrestomanci was coming along the gallery on the other side of the staircase, behind a curly marble balustrade. He was wearing a fawn-colored suit now, instead of the imperial dressing gown, but he looked, if possible, even more elegant. By the look on his face, his thoughts were miles away. Gwendolen ran around the head of the marble staircase and stood herself in front of him. Chrestomanci blinked, and looked vaguely from her to Cat. “Was one of you wanting me?”

“Yes. Me,” said Gwendolen. “Mr. Saunders won’t give me witchcraft lessons, and I want you to tell him he must.”

“Oh, but I can’t do that,” Chrestomanci said absentmindedly. “Sorry and so on.”

words.definition: Extremely intense or extreme in degree.
words.example: Mr. Saunders flew into a towering rage.
words.exampleText: Mr. Saunders flew into a towering rage.

words.definition: To shut or place something down forcefully and loudly.
words.example: He slammed his big, knobby hand down on Cat’s desk.
words.exampleText: He slammed his big, knobby hand down on Cat’s desk.

words.definition: Having small lumps or knobs.
words.example: His big, knobby hand.
words.exampleText: His big, knobby hand.

words.definition: Completely puzzled or confused.
words.example: Cat was very perplexed to find Mr. Saunders angry for such a peculiar reason.
words.exampleText: Cat was very perplexed to find Mr. Saunders angry for such a peculiar reason.

words.definition: Strange or odd; unusual.
words.example: For such a peculiar reason.
words.exampleText: For such a peculiar reason.

words.definition: To say something loudly and forcefully, often with anger.
words.example: He roared, 'Left-handed!'
words.exampleText: He roared, 'Left-handed!'

words.definition: To speak hesitantly or lose strength or momentum.
words.example: Cat faltered, very shaken.
words.exampleText: Cat faltered, very shaken.

words.definition: Feeling confused or baffled upon discovering something.
words.example: Cat was perplexed to find Mr. Saunders was angry for such a peculiar reason.
words.exampleText: Cat was perplexed to find Mr. Saunders was angry for such a peculiar reason.

words.definition: To have earned or be worthy of something.
words.example: They deserve to be tied up in knots and roasted!
words.exampleText: They deserve to be tied up in knots and roasted!

words.definition: Cooked over direct heat or figuratively severely punished.
words.example: They deserve to be tied up in knots and roasted!
words.exampleText: They deserve to be tied up in knots and roasted!

words.definition: Great or impossible to measure damage or injury.
words.example: You’re doing yourself untold harm by obeying them.
words.exampleText: You’re doing yourself untold harm by obeying them.

words.definition: Following commands or rules.
words.example: By obeying them, boy!
words.exampleText: By obeying them, boy!

words.definition: In a way that expresses sadness or sorrow.
words.example: He looked mournfully at his ink-splashed essay.
words.exampleText: He looked mournfully at his ink-splashed essay.

words.definition: Covered or marked with splashes of ink.
words.example: His ink-splashed essay.
words.exampleText: His ink-splashed essay.

words.definition: To remove a page from a book or document by ripping it.
words.example: Mr. Saunders took the book and tore the page right out.
words.exampleText: Mr. Saunders took the book and tore the page right out.

words.definition: A flat object used to carry food or drinks.
words.example: Mary came in with a tray of milk and biscuits.
words.exampleText: Mary came in with a tray of milk and biscuits.

words.definition: To look at something with sadness or regret.
words.example: He looked mournfully at his ink-splashed essay.
words.exampleText: He looked mournfully at his ink-splashed essay.

words.definition: To turn quickly or abruptly.
words.example: Gwendolen whirled around and ran away.
words.exampleText: Gwendolen whirled around and ran away.

words.definition: A long passage in a building from which doors lead into rooms.
words.example: Down the corridor beyond.
words.exampleText: Down the corridor beyond.

words.definition: Impressive in size, appearance, or style.
words.example: A much grander part of the Castle.
words.exampleText: A much grander part of the Castle.

words.definition: A staircase made from marble stone, typically elegant and grand.
words.example: The marble staircase curled away downward.
words.exampleText: The marble staircase curled away downward.

words.definition: Pleasantly graceful and stylish in appearance or manner.
words.example: An elegant dome in the roof.
words.exampleText: An elegant dome in the roof.

words.definition: In a strong, intense, or aggressive manner.
words.example: Gwendolen said fiercely.
words.exampleText: Gwendolen said fiercely.

words.definition: To rise or cause to rise and float in the air, typically by magic or supernatural means.
words.example: It took two of them just to levitate a jug of cocoa!
words.exampleText: It took two of them just to levitate a jug of cocoa!

words.definition: A long passage or corridor, often with an open side or many windows.
words.example: Chrestomanci was coming along the gallery.
words.exampleText: Chrestomanci was coming along the gallery.

words.definition: A railing supported by balusters, especially on a balcony, bridge, or terrace.
words.example: Behind a curly marble balustrade.
words.exampleText: Behind a curly marble balustrade.

words.definition: Having a pale yellowish-tan colour like a young deer (fawn).
words.example: He was wearing a fawn-colored suit.
words.exampleText: He was wearing a fawn-colored suit.

words.definition: A luxurious or majestic robe typically worn indoors by royalty or nobility.
words.example: Instead of the imperial dressing gown, he wore a fawn-colored suit.
words.exampleText: Instead of the imperial dressing gown, he wore a fawn-colored suit.

words.definition: In a distracted or inattentive manner.
words.example: Chrestomanci said absentmindedly.
